english: Ld. soft, mild; also chaste, modest (corrupted from bág-mo (bag mo)?).

1. sunk, settled, v. báb-pa (bab pa); nú-ma-la raṅ-bábs-kyi rdzas byúg-ste (nu ma la rang babs kyi rdzas byug ste) rubbing the breasts with a medicine, so that they sank down of themselves, as if they were full Glr.; bábs - sa (babs sa) settlement, colony Sch. — 2. shape, form, appearance Sch. — 3. rta-babs (rta babs) v. rta (rta), comp.

1. rotten, decayed, putrid, ro bám-pa (ro bam pa) putrid corpse Tar., bam-rô (bam ro), id.; prob. also corpse in general, esp. in connection with sorcery; bam-čén (bam chen), id.? Thgr. — 2. mould, white film on liquids; mouldy, fusty, musty W.

1. bundle of wood or grass Schr., Sch. — 2. division, section, of books, (of greater length than a chapter); in metrical compositions it is said to comprise a number of 300 verses; glegs-bám (glegs bam) v. glegs (glegs); bam-šiṅ (bam shing) Sch. board, prob = glegs-šiṅ (glegs shing).
1. Sch. dull, weak, from old age or long labour, worn out, by much usage. — 2. W. mould.

sbst. (Cs. also bár-ma (bar ma)) 1. intermediate space, interstice, interval, mkhar γnyis-kyi bar zám-gyis sbrél-ba (mkhar gnyis kyi bar zam gyis sbrel ba) Glr. over-bridging the space between the two castles; sa-bár (sa bar) straits, narrow sea; ču-bár (chu bar) isthmus, neck of land; *páṅ-gi bar (pang gi bar), láṅ-ke bar (lang ke bar), čé bar* (che bar) shelf of a repository, cup-board etc. W.; intermediate, middle, mean, stod smad bar γsum (stod smad bar gsum) upper, lower and middle country Ma.; bar _odir (bar 'dir) here in the middle countries Glr.; bár-gyi (bar gyi), id., as adj. Tar. and elsewh.; bár-gyi sder-čágs (bar gyi sder chags), in Wdn. a lizard, as an amphibium partaking of two natures; bár-na (bar na), bár-du (bar du), bár-la (bar la) adv. and postp. c. genit. (and accus.), lám-gyi bár-na (lam gyi bar na) in the middle of the road (there is a well); on the road, in or on the way, on the journey Dzl.; brág-bar btsir-ba (brag bar btsir ba) to be squeezed between two rocks Thgy.; ló-ma daṅ yál-gai bár-du (lo ma dang yal ga'i bar du) between leaves and branches Dzl.; rgya bod bár-la gró-bai mi (rgya bod bar la gro ba'i mi) people travelling between China and Tibet Glr.; deï bár-la (de'i bar la), de-bár (de bar) between Glr.; in the mean time, at the same time, Glr.; zla-ba γsum-gyi bár-du (zla ba gsum gyi bar du) (to provide for a person) for the space of 3 months Dzl.; žag bdún-gyi bár-du (zhag bdun gyi bar du) for seven days (he had not eaten any thing) Dzl.; tuṅ-čiṅ byá-bai sâ-ča bár-du byon (tung ching bya ba'i sa cha bar du byon) he went as far as the country called tuṅ-čiṅ (tung ching) Glr.; dá-ltaï bár-du (da lta'i bar du) Glr., da-tsam-gyi bár-du (da tsam gyi bar du) Dzl.; da-bár (da bar), Mil. until now, hitherto; de(i) bár(du) (de(i) bar(du)) id., when referring to what is past until then; _obrás-buï bár-du tób-pa ('bras bu'i bar du tob pa) to obtain all, even to the fruit (inclusive of the fruit) Dzl.; lan γsum-gyi bár-du (lan gsum gyi bar du) at three (different) times Dzl.; frq. with verbs: rtsé-mo-la túg-gi bár-du (rtse mo la tug gi bar du) till even touching the top Dzl. and so frq.; rel. to time gen. with a negative, being then equivalent to as long as, ma tób-pai bár-du (ma tob pa'i bar du) as long as it has not been obtained = until its having been obtained Dzl.; ṅa ma śi bár-du (nga ma shi bar du) till or up to my death Mil.; ma bsleb bár-du (ma bsleb bar du) as long as we have not reached, attained Glr.; seldom without a negation: mya-ṅán-las _odás-pai bár-du (mya ngan las 'das pa'i bar du) Dzl. LV, 4 (s. l. c.); bár-nas (bar nas) from between, rtsíb-mai bár-nas (rtsib ma'i bar nas) from between the ribs Glr. — 2. fig. bar byéd-pa (bar byed pa) to interpose, intercede, mediate Glr., cf. bár-mi (bar mi). — 3. Termin. of ba (ba), and cf. par (par) III. Comp. and deriv. *bhár-kya* (bhar kya) partition-wall C. — bar-skabs (bar skabs) space of time, period Tar. — bar-skór (bar skor) veranda, exterior gallery of the middle story of a house. — bar-khaṅ (bar khang) Sch. a building between two other houses; Schr. a room between two others. — bar-gós (bar gos) Schr. waist-coat. — bár-ga (bar ga) some, several; several times, now ... now ... Dzl. — bar-čód (bar chod), -čad (-chad), perh. also -γčod (-gchod), sbst. to bar-du γčod-pa (bar du gchod pa), (v. γčod-pa (gchod pa)) hinderance, impediment; danger; damage, failure, fatal accident; tsé-la bar-čád _ooṅ (tshe la bar chad 'ong), or byuṅ (byung) (my) life is in danger; lús-kyi (lus kyi), sróg-gi bar-čád-du _ogyur (srog gi bar chad du 'gyur) id.; also: to meet with an accident, to perish, to be lost Dzl. and elsewh.; *bar-čad-la śi* (bar chad la shi) W., he met with a violent death; bar-čád sél-ba (bar chad sel ba) to protect against fatal accidents, of magic spells frq.; ṅá-la bar-čád méd-par (nga la bar chad med par) without meeting with an accident Mil.; bar-čád rtsóm-pa (bar chad rtsom pa) to meditate evil, to brood<
